commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `nativeModules: ["openclaw"]` to plugin-loader jiti options and asserts that option in the plugin SDK alias test.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +1. Total +4 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. I did not rerun the marker-based CLI reproduction against current main. The source path is clear: current main lacks `nativeModules`, and jiti's 2.7.0 contract confirms that option is what forces native loading for modules under `node_modules`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR has no narrow automation repair target, but it is draft and protected by the maintainer label, so the next step is maintainer readiness and merge judgment.

target.title: fix(plugins): keep openclaw chunks native in jiti
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `nativeModules: ["openclaw"]` to the plugin-loader jiti options so jiti-loaded plugin entries keep OpenClaw's own package chunks on the native module graph instead of evaluating them again in jiti's cache.
- Add a regression assertion for the plugin loader native boundary.
- This is a narrow replacement for the intended fix in #85058; it intentionally excludes the release/version/docs/CI churn present on that branch.

Closes #85057.
Supersedes #85058.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- AWS Crabbox `run_ca70839d20e9` / lease `cbx_0c7c6bf50d17`: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts && git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** OpenClaw's plugin loader now configures jiti to keep `node_modules/openclaw/...` paths on native require/import when jiti transforms a plugin entry, preventing OpenClaw's own dist chunks from being evaluated again in jiti's separate cache.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gwt branch on current `origin/main`, plus an AWS Crabbox Linux runner (`cbx_0c7c6bf50d17`, run `run_ca70839d20e9`) synced from this branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts && ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/sdk-alias.ts src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts && git diff --check"
```

**Evidence after fix:** Local `sdk-alias` Vitest passed 71/71. Formatting and whitespace checks passed. Autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings. AWS Crabbox `run_ca70839d20e9` passed the same focused Vitest, format, and whitespace proof with exit code 0; lease `cbx_0c7c6bf50d17` stopped cleanly.

**Observed result after fix:** `buildPluginLoaderJitiOptions({})` returns `nativeModules: ["openclaw"]` while preserving `tryNative: true`, `interopDefault: true`, and the existing `.js`/`.ts` extension support. Existing source-transform alias coverage in `src/plugins/sdk-alias.test.ts` remains green.

**What was not tested:** I did not mutate a globally installed npm package with marker side effects as in the original issue reproduction. The PR instead locks the documented jiti option at source level and verifies the full plugin SDK alias test file locally and on remote Crabbox.
target.author: vincentkoc
